description: Four-stage data pipeline over Lean proofs: Stage A (forward expert transitions), Stage B (perturb a correct proof -> failure + counterfactual transitions with a faithful Lean error_class), Stage C (block-compression macro edits), Stage D (round-trip forward/inverse pairs for a cycle-consistency loss). Schema-v2 records (state_before, perturbation, state_after, lean_feedback); theorem-disjoint splits; backed by a vendored, built Lean REPL.
motivation: Build the (state, edit, next-state, feedback) dataset - including failure dynamics and macro edits - that no existing Lean corpus provides, and validate the ITP-as-RL state/action design.
formal_results: Runs end-to-end on a real built Lean REPL: 66 schema-valid records from 6 standalone fixture proofs (train 33 / val 18 / test_id 15), theorem-disjoint, zero orphan round-trip pairs; outcomes solved/error/still_valid with an error_class taxonomy; 101 tests pass. Fixture scale only (target 150k-500k low-level + 20k-60k macro).
results_description: The pipeline produces schema-valid transition records including real failure transitions; the ITP-as-RL state/action design is implemented and exercised at toy scale.
conclusions: ITP-as-RL is operational at the data layer (states, edit-actions, failure dynamics all capturable). Scale-up (Mathlib extraction) is blocked on disk; the model, baseline, planner, and eval are not yet built.

comments.provenance: The 66-record fixture dataset is gitignored (present on disk, not committed); counts come from src/data/shard_stats, not re-derivable from git. The data is standalone-Lean fixtures, not Mathlib.
